name: Spaghetti Bolognese
instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion, carrot, celery, and garlic and sauté until softened.
2. Add ground beef and cook until browned.
3. Add canned tomatoes, tomato paste, beef broth, red wine, dried oregano, and dried basil. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
4. Bring sauce to a boil, then reduce heat and let simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Cook spaghetti according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and return to pot.
6. Add spaghetti to the pot with the sauce and toss to coat. Serve hot.

name: Chicken Alfredo
instructions:
1. Cook fettuccine according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
2. Season chicken breasts with salt and black pepper. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook chicken until browned on both sides and cooked through. Remove from skillet and set aside.
3. In the same skillet, melt butter over medium heat. Add garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
4. Add heavy cream and parmesan cheese and stir until cheese is melted and sauce is heated through.
5. Slice chicken and add to the skillet with the sauce. Cook for 2-3 minutes until chicken is heated through.
6. Serve fettuccine topped with chicken and sauce.

name: Beef Stroganoff
instructions:
1. Cook egg noodles according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
2. Cut sirloin steak into thin strips. Season with salt and black pepper.
3. Melt butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add steak and cook until browned on all sides. Remove from skillet and set aside.
4. Add chopped onion and sliced mushrooms to the same skillet. Cook until mushrooms are browned and onion is tender.
5. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
6. Add flour and stir until mixture is well combined.
7. Slowly pour in beef broth, stirring constantly, until mixture comes to a simmer and thickens.
8. Return steak to the skillet and stir to combine with the mushroom sauce.
9. Remove from heat and stir in sour cream.
10. Serve beef stroganoff over egg noodles.

name: Chicken Parmesan
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Pound chicken breasts until they are an even thickness.
3. Season chicken breasts with salt and black pepper.
4. Set up three shallow dishes: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, and one with a mixture of breadcrumbs and grated Parmesan cheese.
5. Dredge each chicken breast in flour, shaking off any excess. Dip in beaten eggs, then coat with breadcrumb mixture.
6. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add breaded chicken breasts and cook until golden brown on both sides.
7. Spread marinara sauce over the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Place chicken breasts on top of the sauce.
8. Spoon additional marinara sauce over the chicken. Sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese over the top.
9. Bake for 25-30 minutes, until chicken is cooked through and cheese is melted and bubbly.
10. Serve with pasta, garlic bread, or a green salad.

name: Grilled Chicken Salad
instructions:
1. Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
2. Season chicken breasts with salt and black pepper on both sides.
3. Grill chicken breasts for 6-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through. Let cool slightly, then slice into strips.
4. In a large bowl, combine chopped romaine lettuce, halved cherry tomatoes, sliced cucumber, sliced red onion, and diced avocado.
5. In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, honey, salt, and black pepper.
6. Add the grilled chicken strips to the bowl with the salad ingredients, then drizzle the dressing over everything. Toss to combine.
7. Serve immediately, garnished with additional lemon wedges if desired.

name: Baked Salmon
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Grease a baking dish with cooking spray and place the salmon fillets in the dish.
3. Season the salmon with salt and black pepper.
4. In a small bowl, mix together the minced garlic and melted butter. Spoon the garlic butter over the salmon fillets.
5. Place the sliced lemon over the salmon fillets.
6. Bake in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until the salmon flakes easily with a fork.
7. Remove from the oven and serve immediately.

name: Butternut Squash Soup
instructions:
1. In a large pot, heat the butter and olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrot, celery, and minced garlic. Sauté for 5-7 minutes, or until the vegetables are softened.
2. Add the cubed butternut squash to the pot, along with the vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the squash is tender.
3. Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth. Alternatively, let the soup cool slightly and blend in batches in a blender.
4. Stir in the heavy cream, and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
5. Heat the soup over low heat until warmed through. Serve hot, garnished with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of black pepper if desired.

name: New England Clam Chowder
instructions:
1. In a large pot, cook the diced bacon over medium heat until crispy. Remove the bacon from the pot with a slotted spoon and set aside.
2. Add the diced onion and celery to the pot with the bacon fat. Sauté for 5-7 minutes, or until the vegetables are softened.
3. Add the minced garlic and sauté for another 1-2 minutes.
4. Add the diced potatoes, clam juice, bay leaf, thyme, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Bring to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
5. Stir in the heavy cream and canned clams. Heat through but do not boil.
6. Remove the bay leaf and serve hot, garnished with the reserved crispy bacon.
